posts.author: RahulChoudhary
posts.text: Regarding MCX Gold and Silver today — I am a bit confused. The i-NAV was 136 for Goldbees today at around 09:16 AM But now, it is updated to 131.49 at 12:04 PM, same as Goldbees market price, so 0% premium/discount. What is going on? Import duty hiked by 9%, so the i-Nav should also increase, right? If yes, why it is 131.52 right now? If no, why was it 136 at 09:16 AM? Same thing happened for Silvercase, Goldcase etc. What changed?

posts.author: Sankalp
posts.text: Regarding MCX Gold and Silver today — Some of the impact was likely already priced in, and futures don’t move 1:1 with duty changes since traders also factor in lower demand, arbitrage, and existing domestic premium over spot.

posts.author: ashwinraghavan
posts.text: Regarding MCX Gold and Silver today — Is there any literature on how these margins are calculated? Asking purely out of academic interest

posts.author: Ragavendran_M
posts.text: Regarding MCX Gold and Silver today — Margins have been increased in the exchange’s first intraday span due to market movement. Hence, margins are increased to avoid risk due to volatility.

posts.author: Singrauli
posts.text: Regarding MCX Gold and Silver today — @Adarsh_Patil

posts.author: dcd
posts.text: Regarding MCX Gold and Silver today — Hi all, Two questions: Duty increased by 9% on Gold and Silver, but the contracts are only up around 6%. Why? Was there already a premium over spot for this eventuality? Few minutes ago, Zerodha has drastically applied higher margins on these contracts. Dhan is still the same margin. Let me know if this is exchange or broker applied increase in margin? thanks
